title: AirBnB donating more than 1,100 free World Cup tickets for Mass. youth, Healey announces
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: World Cup “These tickets will help make sure that kids who otherwise may never have the opportunity to attend a match can experience the excitement of the world's biggest sporting event,” Healey said.
More than one thousand Massachusetts children will be able to attend World Cup games for free through tickets donated by AirBnB, officials announced.

AirBnB is donating 1,104 World Cup tickets to the state’s Boys & Girls Clubs, the Department of Children and Families (DCF), and community groups in the state, the office of Governor Maura Healey said in a press release Monday.

title: Red Sox 2004 World Series champion doesn’t want Boston to ‘blow it up yet’
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Red Sox "That staff, with [Aroldis] Chapman at the back end, can win a World Series.
" Kevin Millar won a World Series with the Red Sox in 2004.

title: Newly passed bill removes ‘offensive language’ from Mass. General Laws
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Politics “This legislation is our latest effort to ensure that our state laws do not use antiquated words that carry negative connotations, words that also serve as a reminder of past injustices.
” ShareSend this article to your social connections.
The Massachusetts Legislature has approved a bill that would remove what lawmakers describe as “outdated and offensive language” used to describe people with disabilities in the state’s General Laws.

title: Kayshon Boutte reacts to Patriots trading for A.J. Brown
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: New England Patriots "You watch him, he dominates.
Great X-receiver, dominant.
" Kayshon Boutte will be at Patriots minicamp on Tuesday.

title: 4 families displaced by Winthrop fire; resident gives birth shortly after
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Local News The fire's cause remains unknown, though investigators noted a hybrid car was parked in between the two homes.
A fire that engulfed two homes in Winthrop Sunday displaced four families, including one resident who gave birth to a baby girl shortly after being evacuated, authorities said.
The Winthrop Fire Department responded to a reported house fire at 155-157 River Road shortly before 4:30 p.

title: WCVB reporter Matt Reed to leave station
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Local News Reed is a longtime reporter who won back-to-back Edward R.
Murrow Awards in recent years.
Longtime WCVB reporter Matt Reed announced recently that he is departing the station, making him the latest in a string of Boston-area TV journalists leaving their stations.

title: NBA Insider hasn’t heard ‘one iota’ about Jaylen Brown being available for trade
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Boston Celtics "I'm just going to tell you that I haven't heard any material true discussions.
" Jaylen Brown during the Celtics' playoff series against Joel Embiid and the 76ers.
Danielle Parhizkaran/Globe Staff As the Celtics mull potential personnel changes during the offseason, there has been no shortage of rumors about moves they could make.

title: The Cape Cod bridges are up for adoption
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Local News The iconic Bourne and Sagamore bridges are free to take, but come with restrictions.
The Sagamore Bridge spans the Cape Cod Canal.
John Tlumacki / The Boston Globe, File The iconic Cape Cod bridges are looking for new homes.

title: Waltham man charged with manslaughter after deadly road rage incident
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Crime Timothy Scalisi and Nicholas Dagostino allegedly exited their vehicles and fought during rush hour in Waltham.
Dagostino suffered a skull fracture and later died.

title: Michael Proctor denied last-minute deposition delay after Karen Read drops explosive new suit
author: Abby Patkin
